0ada881b-2f54-445f-8a95-69a79c91b8bd.title: Backend: Add RunShellCommand/StopShellCommand to SystemService with event streaming
0ada881b-2f54-445f-8a95-69a79c91b8bd.details:
## Files
- **New** `internal/services/shell_command.go`
- **Modify** `internal/services/events.go`
- **Modify** `internal/webserver/server.go`

## Work
- Define `ShellCommandEvent` struct: `{ WorkspaceID, ID, Type ("started"|"stdout"|"stderr"|"completed"), Data any }`
- Add `shellCommandRuns map[string]context.CancelFunc` to `SystemService` (protected by `chatMu`, keyed by run ID)
- **`RunShellCommand(workspaceID, command, workingDirectory string, timeoutSeconds, maxOutputBytes int) (string, error)`**
  - Validate workspace and working directory (reuse tool logic from `internal/tools`)
  - Generate unique run ID (`workspaceID:seq`)
  - Store cancel func in `shellCommandRuns`
  - Emit `"started"` event
  - Goroutine runs `exec.CommandContext` via `shellCommandInvocation`, pipes stdout/stderr through `bufio.Scanner`, emits per-line `"stdout"`/`"stderr"` events
  - On completion, emit `"completed"` with exitCode, timedOut, durationMilliseconds; clean up map entry
- **`StopShellCommand(workspaceID, runID string) error`** — cancels and removes from map
- Export `ShellRuntimeEventName = "echo:shell:event"` constant in `events.go`
- Add `"RunShellCommand": true` and `"StopShellCommand": true` to `allowedRPCMethods`
- Run `wails generate`
acceptanceCriteria: go test ./... passes
RunShellCommand returns run ID immediately and streams per-line stdout/stderr events via echo:shell:event
StopShellCommand cancels a running command and removes it from the map

1954996a-cff3-48c5-8af7-93490eb110e7.title: Render task references in chat messages
1954996a-cff3-48c5-8af7-93490eb110e7.details:
When a user sends a message containing `@task:<id>`, render it as a clickable/styled reference in the chat log so it's visually distinct from plain text (similar to how `@path` file mentions are rendered).

**Scope:**
- Parse `@task:<id>` patterns in user and assistant message content during rendering
- Render as a styled chip or link element (e.g., `<span class="chat-task-ref">`) showing the task title if resolvable, falling back to the ID
- Clicking the reference could navigate to the task view with that task selected

**Files to change:**
- `echo/frontend/src/app/chat/index.ts` — message rendering logic
- `echo/frontend/src/styles.css` — styling for the task reference element
acceptanceCriteria: @task:<id> patterns in chat messages render as styled references
Reference shows task title when resolvable from the current workspace's task board
Clicking a task reference navigates to task view with that task selected

2594572f-7ae7-456c-87d0-4aad4cf01b1b.title: Fix desktop terminal panel getting cut off at bottom of screen
2594572f-7ae7-456c-87d0-4aad4cf01b1b.details:
## Problem
On desktop, the terminal panel gets cut off at the bottom of the viewport. The `max-height: 60vh` on `.terminal-panel` doesn't account for the space already consumed by the chat input area and other panels above it, so when scrolled to the bottom the terminal's input row is hidden behind the browser/app edge.

## Root Cause
`.terminal-panel` at line 9192 uses `max-height: 60vh` which can extend beyond the available viewport space when combined with the chat composer, left nav, and other UI chrome above it.

## Fix — `frontend/src/styles.css`

Change `.terminal-panel` (line 9192):
- Replace `max-height: 60vh` with `max-height: calc(80vh - 200px)` to account for the chat composer and header space above, or use a fixed pixel cap like `max-height: 500px` that fits within typical desktop viewports.
- Ensure `.terminal-content` has `overflow-y: auto` so internal scrolling works when content exceeds the panel height.

## Verification
- Open terminal on desktop with multiple command runs visible
- Scroll to bottom — input row should be fully visible without being cut off by viewport edge
acceptanceCriteria: Terminal panel input row is fully visible at bottom of screen on desktop
Terminal content scrolls internally when it exceeds available space
No regression on mobile terminal layout

5dd2f461-225e-498b-ad59-56913965f0cf.title: Backend: Add SavedCommand type and CRUD methods to SystemService
5dd2f461-225e-498b-ad59-56913965f0cf.details:
## Files
- **Modify** `internal/services/system.go`
- **Modify** `internal/services/state_persistence.go`
- **Modify** `internal/webserver/server.go`

## Work

### `system.go` — New type and AppState field

Add type:
```go
type SavedCommand struct {
	ID      string `json:"id"`
	Name    string `json:"name"`
	Command string `json:"command"`
	Order   int    `json:"order"`
}
```

Add to `AppState`:
```go
SavedCommands map[string][]SavedCommand `json:"savedCommands,omitempty"`
```

### `system.go` — 3 exported methods

**`GetSavedCommands(workspaceID string) []SavedCommand`**
- Validate workspace exists via `workspaceAndSettings`
- Return deep clone of `s.state.SavedCommands[workspaceID]` (empty slice if none)

**`UpsertSavedCommand(workspaceID, id, name, command string, order int) error`**
- Validate workspace exists
- Trim inputs; reject if name and command both empty
- If id is empty, generate UUID (`uuid.New().String()`)
- Lock `s.mu`, clone existing slice for workspace, find-by-id to update or append new
- Call `s.saveLocked()`

**`DeleteSavedCommand(workspaceID, id string) error`**
- Validate workspace exists
- Lock `s.mu`, filter out matching id from workspace's slice
- Call `s.saveLocked()`

### `state_persistence.go` — Clone support

Update the state clone helper to deep-copy `SavedCommands`:
```go
dst.SavedCommands = make(map[string][]SavedCommand, len(src.SavedCommands))
for k, v := range src.SavedCommands {
    dst.SavedCommands[k] = append([]SavedCommand{}, v...)
}
```

### `webserver/server.go` — RPC whitelist

Add to `allowedRPCMethods`:
```go
"GetSavedCommands":     true,
"UpsertSavedCommand":   true,
"DeleteSavedCommand":   true,
```

## Verification
- `go test ./...` passes
acceptanceCriteria: SavedCommand struct exported from services package with ID, Name, Command, Order fields
AppState has SavedCommands map[string][]SavedCommand field persisted to state.json
GetSavedCommands returns deep clone of workspace's saved commands
UpsertSavedCommand creates new (with UUID) or updates existing command
DeleteSavedCommand removes command by id from workspace's slice
All 3 methods whitelisted in webserver RPC allow-list
go test ./... passes

77591a51-2a2c-488b-be42-5fc1c21b0187.title: Add ConsoleOutput field to ChatToolActivity and populate for shell_command
77591a51-2a2c-488b-be42-5fc1c21b0187.details:

### 1. Add field to struct (line ~37)
```go
type ChatToolActivity struct {
    ID            string `json:"id"`
    Name          string `json:"name,omitempty"`
    Arguments     string `json:"arguments,omitempty"`
    Status        string `json:"status"`

```

## Changes to `echo/internal/services/chat.go` - format helper

## Verification

85f77ab6-82f0-4056-bcc2-96ae43c42c64.title: Implement Saved Commands UI in Terminal Panel
85f77ab6-82f0-4056-bcc2-96ae43c42c64.details:
## Problem
Backend SavedCommand type and CRUD methods are fully implemented but no frontend code exists to expose them. Users cannot save, view, run, edit, or delete saved commands.

## Backend Reference (already done)
- `SavedCommand` struct: `{ID, Name, Command, Order}` in `internal/services/system.go:152`
- `AppState.SavedCommands map[string][]SavedCommand` persisted to state.json
- `GetSavedCommands(workspaceID string) []SavedCommand` at line 339
- `UpsertSavedCommand(workspaceID, id, name, command string, order int) error` at line 351
- `DeleteSavedCommand(workspaceID, id string) error` at line 386
- Wails bindings generated in `frontend/wailsjs/go/services/SystemService.d.ts` (lines 75, 103, 273)

## Files to Modify

### 1. `frontend/src/backend/services.ts` — Service Wrappers
Add 3 functions following the existing `call()` pattern:
```ts
export function GetSavedCommands(...args: Parameters<typeof Wails.GetSavedCommands>): ReturnType<typeof Wails.GetSavedCommands> {
  return call("GetSavedCommands", Wails.GetSavedCommands, args);
}
export function UpsertSavedCommand(...args: Parameters<typeof Wails.UpsertSavedCommand>): ReturnType<typeof Wails.UpsertSavedCommand> {
  return call("UpsertSavedCommand", Wails.UpsertSavedCommand, args);
}
export function DeleteSavedCommand(...args: Parameters<typeof Wails.DeleteSavedCommand>): ReturnType<typeof Wails.DeleteSavedCommand> {
  return call("DeleteSavedCommand", Wails.DeleteSavedCommand, args);
}
```

### 2. `frontend/src/app/state.ts` — State Fields
Add to the state object near terminal-related state (after `terminalOpen`):
```ts
savedCommands: new Map<string, services.SavedCommand[]>(),
savedCommandEditingId: "",
savedCommandDraftName: "",
savedCommandDraftCommand: "",
savedCommandOpenSections: new Set<string>(),
```

### 3. `frontend/src/app/render.ts` — Terminal Panel Render
In `renderTerminalPanel()` at line 521, insert a collapsible "Saved Commands" section between `.terminal-runs` and `.terminal-input-row`:
- `<details>` with summary toggling via `toggle-saved-commands` action
- List of saved commands showing name + truncated command text
- Each row has Run (▶), Edit (✎), Delete (✕) icon buttons
- "+ Save current" button at bottom that captures the terminal input value
- When editing, replace a row with inline form: name input + command input + Save/Cancel

Add helper function `renderSavedCommandItem(sc: services.SavedCommand, workspaceId: string): string`.

### 4. `frontend/src/app/actions.ts` — Action Handlers
Add handlers for these actions in the delegation handler:
- `toggle-saved-commands`: Toggle workspace ID in/out of `savedCommandOpenSections`, re-render
- `run-saved-command`: Look up command from saved list, set `terminalDrafts`, programmatically trigger run or fill input and render
- `add-saved-command`: Read `[data-terminal-input]` value; if non-empty, extract name (first word), set editing state with draft values, re-render to show inline form
- `edit-saved-command`: Set `savedCommandEditingId`, populate drafts from existing command, re-render
- `delete-saved-command`: Call `DeleteSavedCommand(workspaceID, id)`, clear edit state if needed, re-render
- `save-edited-command`: Validate name+command non-empty, call `UpsertSavedCommand` with existing or new ID (generate UUID for new), clear editing state, refresh saved commands from backend, re-render

### 5. `frontend/src/app/bootstrap.ts` — Bootstrap Load
After `LoadState()` at line 62, load saved commands for each workspace:
```ts
for (const ws of (state.appState?.workspaces ?? [])) {
  const cmds = await GetSavedCommands(ws.id);
  if (cmds.length > 0) state.savedCommands.set(ws.id, cmds);
}
```

### 6. `frontend/src/styles.css` — Styles
Add styles for:
- `.terminal-saved-commands` — collapsible section with border-top separator
- `.terminal-saved-header summary` — clickable toggle with muted text
- `.terminal-saved-list` — flex column gap layout
- `.terminal-saved-item` — flex row with name label, truncated command text, action buttons
- `.terminal-saved-name` — bold, min-width 80px, ellipsis overflow
- `.terminal-saved-cmd` — mono font, muted color, flex:1, ellipsis
- `.terminal-saved-edit-form` — inline form with name+command inputs side by side
- All interactive elements `min-height: 36px` for mobile tap targets
- Mobile media query: items wrap, command text goes full-width below buttons

### 7. `internal/webserver/server.go` — RPC Whitelist
Add to `allowedRPCMethods`:
```go
"GetSavedCommands":     true,
"UpsertSavedCommand":   true,
"DeleteSavedCommand":   true,
```

## UX Flow
1. User types a command in terminal input
2. User clicks "+ Save current" → inline form appears pre-filled with name (first word of command) and full command
3. User edits name if desired, clicks Save → command persists and appears in saved list
4. User clicks ▶ on any saved command → fills terminal input and runs it
5. User clicks ✎ → switches to inline edit form for that command
6. User clicks ✕ → deletes the command immediately

## Verification
- Open terminal panel → see "Saved Commands" collapsible section
- Type a command, click "+ Save current", save → appears in list
- Click ▶ → command runs through terminal
- Click ✎ → edit inline, save → updates
- Click ✕ → deletes
- Restart app → saved commands persist
- Mobile: tap targets ≥36px, layout wraps properly
- `cd frontend; npm run build` passes
- `go test ./...` passes
acceptanceCriteria: Saved Commands collapsible section renders in terminal panel between command history and input row
Clicking '+ Save current' captures the current terminal input and opens an inline edit form with name/command fields pre-filled
Saving a new command persists it via UpsertSavedCommand and shows it in the saved commands list
Clicking the Run button (▶) on a saved command fills the terminal input and executes it
Clicking Edit (✎) switches a saved command row to an inline edit form with Save/Cancel
Saving an edited command updates it via UpsertSavedCommand and exits edit mode
Clicking Delete (✕) removes the command via DeleteSavedCommand
Saved commands persist across app restarts (loaded from backend in bootstrap)
All 3 methods whitelisted in webserver RPC allow-list for web access
Mobile tap targets are at least 36px on all saved command action buttons
cd frontend; npm run build passes without errors
go test ./... passes

a364b259-7865-434b-9569-e0623468f1c4.title: Re-enable toast notifications on mobile by removing viewport check in pushToast
a364b259-7865-434b-9569-e0623468f1c4.details:
## Problem
On mobile (≤720px), all toast notifications are silently suppressed by a blanket early return in `pushToast`. This makes saved command save/delete/error feedback invisible, and breaks feedback for any other action that uses toasts.

## Fix
**File:** `frontend/src/app/toasts.ts`

Remove these 3 lines from `pushToast`:
```typescript
  if (window.innerWidth <= 720) {
    return;
  }
```

The mobile CSS already positions `.toast-region` at `bottom: 82px` inside `@media (max-width: 720px)` to sit above the fixed bottom nav, so toasts are styled correctly for mobile. The suppression was added in commit `5fd0572` as an overly broad fix.

## Verification
- `cd frontend; npm run build` — confirm it compiles
- Test on mobile viewport (≤720px): click "Save current" with text in terminal → dialog appears, save → toast shows "Command saved."
- Test on mobile: click "Save current" with empty input → toast shows "Enter a command in the terminal first."
acceptanceCriteria: Toast notifications display on mobile viewports (≤720px)
"Save current" with empty input shows error toast on mobile
"Command saved." success toast appears after saving on mobile
Toasts position correctly above the fixed bottom nav bar on mobile
`cd frontend; npm run build` passes

Visual style matches existing app design language

ac43ea4f-829a-4106-b9ac-f18a59d99ffd.title: Add "Add workspace" button to mobile workspace dropdown
ac43ea4f-829a-4106-b9ac-f18a59d99ffd.details:
## Problem On mobile (≤720px), there is no way to add a new workspace. The desktop sidebar dropdown includes an "Add workspace" menu item, but the mobile bottom nav workspace dropdown does not. Additionally, when zero workspaces exist, the mobile nav pill is hidden entirely, leaving no entry point. ## Root cause `renderMobileBottomNav()` in `frontend/src/app/render.ts` (~line 459) renders only existing workspace options inside `.mobile-nav-workspace-dropdown`. It omits the "Add workspace" button that the desktop dropdown includes. When `workspaces.length === 0`, the pill is not rendered at all (`workspaces.length > 0 && workspace` guard). ## Changes ### 1. `frontend/src/app/render.ts` — Mobile dropdown "Add workspace" entry Inside `.mobile-nav-workspace-dropdown`, after the workspace options loop, add a divider and an "Add workspace" button matching the desktop pattern: ```html
``` ### 2. `frontend/src/app/render.ts` — Zero-workspace pill When `workspaces.length === 0`, render a pill that triggers `data-action="add-workspace"` instead of hiding the pill entirely: ```typescript ${workspaces.length > 0 && workspace ? `` : `` } ``` ### 3. `frontend/src/styles.css` — Mobile add-workspace pill styling Inside the `@media (max-width: 720px)` block, add styles for `.mobile-nav-add-workspace` to make it visually distinct (e.g., dashed border or accent color) so users recognize it as an action button rather than a workspace name. ## Key files - `frontend/src/app/render.ts` — `renderMobileBottomNav()` function (~line 443–508) - `frontend/src/styles.css` — mobile responsive block - `frontend/src/app/actions.ts` — `add-workspace` handler already exists (line 458), no backend changes needed ## Verification - Open app on mobile viewport (≤720px) with zero workspaces → pill shows "Add" button, tapping opens folder picker - Open app with one+ workspaces → tap workspace pill → dropdown includes "Add workspace" at bottom with divider - `npm run build` passes in `frontend/`
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile workspace dropdown (≤720px) includes an 'Add workspace' menu item at the bottom, separated by a divider, with data-action='add-workspace'
When zero workspaces exist on mobile, the nav brand section renders an 'Add' pill button that triggers the folder picker via data-action='add-workspace'
The add-workspace pill is visually distinct from a normal workspace pill (e.g., dashed border or accent styling)
Tapping 'Add workspace' on mobile opens the OS folder picker and adds the selected folder as a new workspace
Frontend builds without errors: cd frontend; npm run build

d1d64412-6097-4429-94cc-fc3aa4272a25.title: Add "Reference Task" button to task detail view
d1d64412-6097-4429-94cc-fc3aa4272a25.details:
In `echo/frontend/src/app/tasks/index.ts`, add a new button in the `task-detail-footer-actions` div alongside the existing "Use as Chat Prompt" button.

**Button spec:**
- `data-task-action="reference"` with `data-task-id="${escapeAttribute(task.id)}"`
- Icon: link or paperclip icon (use existing icon from `icons.ts`)
- Label: "Reference Task"
- Class: `secondary-button icon-text-button` (match existing buttons in the footer)

**Handler** (new case in `handleTaskAction`):
- Call `formatTaskReference(task)` to produce a compact reference string like `@task:${task.id}`
- Append it to the existing chat draft (with leading space if needed, trailing space after) — do NOT replace existing draft
- Switch to chat mode and focus input (same pattern as existing `chat` action: set `appMode`, `mobileNavView`, `activeChatKanbanTab`, clear `selectedTaskCards`, render, focus `[data-chat-input]`)

**Helper function:**
```ts
function formatTaskReference(task: services.WorkspaceTask): string {
  return `@task:${task.id}`;
}
```

**Files to change:**
- `echo/frontend/src/app/tasks/index.ts` — button HTML, handler case, helper function
acceptanceCriteria: Reference Task button renders in task detail footer alongside Complete and Use as Chat Prompt
Clicking appends @task:<id> to existing chat draft without replacing it
View switches to chat mode and focuses the input textarea
Existing "Use as Chat Prompt" behavior remains unchanged

fc32a1ba-d84f-47ff-91d1-76200e240161.title: Frontend: Add terminal panel with live streaming output to chat view
fc32a1ba-d84f-47ff-91d1-76200e240161.details:
## Files
- **Modify** `frontend/src/app/types.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/app/state.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/backend/services.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/app/bootstrap.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/app/actions.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/app/render.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/app/icons.ts`
- **Modify** `frontend/src/styles.css`

## Work
- **types.ts**: Add `ShellCommandEvent` and `ShellCommandRun` types
- **state.ts**: Add per-workspace `terminalRuns`, `terminalDrafts`, `terminalOpen` state
- **services.ts**: Add `RunShellCommand` and `StopShellCommand` wrappers
- **bootstrap.ts**: Register `"echo:shell:event"` listener that appends lines and updates run status in real time
- **actions.ts**: Handle `run-shell-command`, `stop-shell-command`, `toggle-terminal` actions
- **render.ts**: Add `renderTerminalPanel(workspace)` — collapsible section in chat view showing command history with live-scrolling color-coded stdout/stderr lines, exit code badge, duration, spinner + stop button for running commands, and input row at bottom. Integrate into `renderWorkspacePanels` for mode `"chat"`
- **icons.ts**: Add terminal/console icon SVG
- **styles.css**: Add `.terminal-panel`, `.terminal-run`, `.terminal-line`, `.terminal-stdout`, `.terminal-stderr`, `.terminal-input-row`, `.terminal-exit-badge` styles (monospace font, scrollable, theme-consistent)
acceptanceCriteria: cd frontend; npm run build passes
Terminal panel appears in chat view with collapsible command history
Live line-by-line stdout/stderr output streams as command runs
Stop button cancels running command
Completed commands show exit code and duration
